content: Metabolic syndrome is a cluster of interconnected cardiometabolic risk factors that increase risk for type 2 diabetes and cardiovascular disease. Multiple definitions exist; harmonized criteria (2009) require ≥3 of 5 components: (1) Abdominal obesity: waist circumference ≥102cm men, ≥88cm women (ethnicity-specific cutoffs); (2) Elevated triglycerides: ≥150 mg/dL or on treatment; (3) Reduced HDL cholesterol: <40 mg/dL men, <50 mg/dL women or on treatment; (4) Elevated blood pressure: ≥130/85 mmHg or on antihypertensive therapy; (5) Elevated fasting glucose: ≥100 mg/dL or previously diagnosed T2DM. Pathophysiology centers on insulin resistance, visceral adiposity, chronic inflammation, and endothelial dysfunction. Prevalence ~20-25% globally, increases with age. Individuals with metabolic syndrome have 2-fold increased CVD risk and 5-fold increased diabetes risk.
